title: Tell us you have sold a vehicle
description: Inform the Barbados Licensing Authority when you sell a vehicle, on your own or with co-owners.
summary: Under Section 18 of the Road Traffic Act, if you sell a vehicle for money you must tell the Barbados Licensing Authority. This keeps the Licensing Authority’s record of who owns what up to date.

There is one version of the Sale of Vehicle form for a single owner and one version for joint owners. They ask for the same things – who you are, what the vehicle is, who you sold it to and whether you want to keep your registration number.
You can choose to keep your registration number if you want to put it on a different vehicle later. If you do not say anything, the registration number goes back into the Licensing Authority’s pool.
Bring the registration card or disc with you. The buyer will not be able to register the vehicle in their name until you have completed this step.

items: your full name, address, ID number, telephone number and email
if there is more than one owner, the names and details of every owner
the registration number, engine number and chassis number of the vehicle
the make and model of the vehicle
the full name and address of the person you sold it to
whether you want to keep your registration number

items.name: Registration card or disc
items.note: From the vehicle you have sold.

items.name: Receipt of sale or bill of sale

fees: There is no fee for telling the Licensing Authority you have sold a vehicle.
how_long: The change is recorded the same day.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Sale of Vehicle form (single owner or joint owner version) to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: We have merged the single-owner and joint-owner versions of the Sale of Vehicle form onto one entry page. The BLA may want to keep them as two separate services.
‘Recorded the same day’ is based on common BLA practice. The form does not state a processing time.
Document list (registration card or disc, receipt of sale, photo ID) is inferred from standard BLA practice.
